title: Limonium psilocladum
taxonomy: Limonium psilocladum is classified within the family Plumbaginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the leadworts or plumbagos. This classification places it within the order Plumbaginales and the clade asterids. Limonium psilocladum is a genus of coastal plants, and this classification is consistent with its geographical and ecological characteristics. The classification also reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Plumbaginaceae family. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Limonium psilocladum within the larger plant kingdom.

intro: Limonium psilocladum, also known as Mediterranean sea lavender, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its clusters of small, blue-purple flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress.
